item.title: Beyond Tellerand – Düsseldorf
item.description: Established in 2014, Beyond Tellerand is where design and technology meet. 15 Years Celebration!

item.title: FITC Toronto 2026
item.description: FITC celebrates their 25th anniversary with the theme REBIRTH, exploring the future of everything innovative, technical and creative. Includes hand-picked speakers from around the world and from a variety of backgrounds.

item.title: European Media Art Festival 2026
item.description: Each year EMAF offers its visitors an overview of current artistic productions in film programmes, exhibitions, performances and hybrid formats. Guest-curated projects and retrospectives provide in-depth insights into historical positions and contexts.

item.title: Paramnésico by Desilence
item.description: Load presents a solo exhibition Paramnésico by Desilence, pioneers of visual scenography and live visuals. Paramnésico takes its title from paramnesia, a condition in which memory loses its reliability, and the boundary between lived experience and imagination begins to blur. Memory is approached not as an archive of fixed images,...

item.title: Flux Festival
item.description: Flux is a festival of creativity showcasing bold innovations in moving image and sound as artists experiment with emerging tools and technologies. Thoughtfully curated program of screenings, talks, sensory exhibits, hands-on workshops, and performances. Blum Gallery, 2727 La Cienega Blvd, LA

item.title: Creative Tech New York 2026 Agenda Now Live: Exploring the Future of AI, XR, and Creative Systems
item.description: This year’s agenda brings together leading voices from Meta Reality Labs, Google Creative Lab, Snap, MAC Cosmetics, Estée Lauder, the American Museum of Natural History, OLIVER, Hyperquake, Sundogs, CYCYMYMY, Unreasonable Studios, and ...

item.title: Diriyah Art Futures Open Call – Emerging New Media Artists Programme
item.description: Diriyah Art Futures (DAF), the MENA region's first New Media Arts centre, has announced a call for applications for the third cycle of its Emerging New Media Artists programme.

item.title: Digital Occultism
item.description: Tracing fictions from early internet cultures to contemporary platforms, this book examines how they produce material realities, how politics operate as psychological warfare, and how algorithmic feedback engineers desire.

item.title: Hush – Bringing inanimate objects to life
item.description: Can we use AI more playfully in the physical world? Hush is a toy box that brings inanimate objects to life by connecting physical play with generative AI.

item.title: Dioptricon – Experiments with parallel light
item.description: Dioptricon is a series of experiments using light as a space-forming medium. Instead of spreading out in a radial manner from a source, in Dioptricon light rays are instead arranged in parallel to each other.

item.title: Prometheus Bound – In the pursuit of perpetual energy
item.description: Prometheus Bound probes the gap between clean energy myths and the messy reality of algae cultivation, while also questioning the ethics of biocontrol and the labor of harvesting electricity from living species.

item.title: Kick Drum Circuit Design Guide
item.description: This illustrated guide walks you through designing an analog kick drum circuit from scratch – starting with a simple five-component bridged-t oscillator and building up to a fully featured percussion voice.

item.title: momentum.js: Integrating Generative Art and Timeline-Based Animation
item.description: momentum.js is a toolkit that brings generative art into the timeline of Adobe After Effects.

item.title: Data Critters – Tracing our digital actions through modern scribes
item.description: Data Critters are custom-built mechanical plotters connected to live data feeds from the platforms they map. They trace paths across paper as data is generated, producing an ever-growing, never-finished map of online human activity.

item.title: Designing Questions
item.description: Designing Questions introduces an alternative design pedagogy. At its core are speculative practices, world-building, material engagement, and storytelling, equipping students and practitioners to address urgent questions of our time.

item.title: IED Master of Arts in Interaction Design: shaping emerging technologies for products and services
item.description: IED Master of Arts in Interaction Design focuses on training designers who can shape and manage interaction ecosystems where physical and digital dimensions converge.

item.title: In A Minute – The rhythm of our global impact
item.description: The keys in the front door, the scribbling of a pencil, the bubbling of the coffee pot. These elements of our everyday soundtrack hold meaning for all of us, so we programmed these familiar objects to move to the rhythm of our global impact.

item.title: V6 Development Log (03/2026)
item.description: Activity gets a major update: LiveURLs let you embed p5.js, video, audio and more directly in posts. Custom Themes let you personalise CAN. API v2.3 and iOS v1.5.1 add Analytics and Channels.

item.title: Incomputable Earth: Technology and the Anthropocene Hypothesis
item.description: This open access collection argues that climate breakdown represents an irreducibly incomputable problem that cannot be resolved through algorithmic optimization or cybernetic planetary management.

item.title: Maintenance of Everything
item.description: Maintenance of Everything is an in-depth exploration of maintenance—and a powerful argument for its civilizational importance.

item.title: Paramnésico by Desilence at Load Gallery
item.description: Load gallery celebrates its second birthday with a solo exhibition Paramnésico by Desilence, pioneers of visual scenography and real-time motion video.

item.title: Uncoded Gestures – Tracing the echos of the past
item.description: Uncoded Gestures is an installation that traces cultural memory through posture. By mapping live silhouettes to archival photos, it reveals how modern bodies echo the past, turning everyday gestures into a dialogue across time.

item.title: Partition – You look through a wall, the wall looks through you
item.description: Partition is a pen plotter that draws at the speed of your heartbeat, sensed through a gallery wall without your consent.

item.title: Beyond the Screen – How algorithms look back at us
item.description: Beyond the Screen is an immersive installation that explores how algorithms look right back at us - exposing, in real time, how machine vision perceives and interprets human behaviour.

item.title: Material Interactions – A New Species of Design
item.description: In a debut collaboration, a series of five material-driven kinetic sculptures by designers Nadya Suvorova and Heidi Jalkh don’t just move: they respond, proving that "passive matter" is anything but.

item.title: Forever Frequencies – Joint symphony of sound and reminiscence
item.description: Forever Frequencies is an installation that computes a personal melody from a visitor's memory, using their own past and imagined future as the raw material for a one-of-a-kind musical composition.

item.title: Helical Echo – A modular, evolving object
item.description: The Helical Echo is a sculptural totem created for CultHoldings. Like the abstracted DNA forms it displays, the sculpture itself is designed to evolve, to be upgraded and reshaped.

item.title: Image Empire – An animated fairytale about the fusion of the real and the virtual
item.description: URLalanwarburton.co.uk

item.title: T(h)ree – Integrating community knowledge with natural infrastructure and AI
item.description: T(h)ree is a permanent landscape intervention in Central London, that gives a ‘voice’ to a Tree that has witnessed hundreds of years of history, so people can talk with it and ask questions.

item.title: Scruple – A Chrome extension that replaces moral hesitation with computation
item.description: Scruple reads your Gmail, runs four ethical frameworks in parallel, and writes the replies — simulating compassion, automating principle, outsourcing guilt.

item.title: Interview with Marc Vilanova – The hidden frequencies of embodied sound experiences
item.description: Serena Cangiano meets Marc Vilanova to talk about infrasound, embodied listening, and the invisible frequencies that connect human bodies, urban infrastructures, and natural environments.

item.title: Corpseware – Seven years of dormancy, one moment of bloom
item.description: Corpseware is a minimal, disc based software ritual housed in a tablet form vernacular shrine inspired by the bloom mechanic of the Corpse Flower.

item.title: Residual Lines – Fragility of Knowledge
item.description: Residual Lines is a redaction machine that visualizes the loss of new knowledge tied to recent research grant cuts.

item.title: Exploring Post-Poster at MDes Basel
item.description: Grace Hannah Park’s graduate thesis project at MDes Basel reveals new possibilities for the moving poster through an exploration of physical sensors and creative-coding.

item.title: StarScript – Collective wonder encoded, processed, and projected
item.description: StarScript is an interactive astronomy-inspired installation that inverts the observational logic of radio telescopes — turning the act of image reconstruction into a live, collective, and tactile experience.

item.title: Analytic Scape – World as classified and quantified by AI
item.description: Analytic Scape is a series of scape videos reconstructed through the eye of AI. After capturing various urban and natural scenes, the footage was analyzed using twenty distinct computer vision algorithms.

item.title: Light Streaks – Generative photography
item.description: The latest in the series of experiments by Karsten Schmidt, is a small selection of long-exposure images created from randomly generated IFS, a family of very oldskool primitive/trivial fractal functions

item.title: Zero Range – Documenting the unbearable in a culture of systematic filtering
item.description: Zero Range is an interactive installation that exposes the administrative violence of data that is recorded but ignored. It highlights how available information about violence vanishes through systematic disregard and filtering.

item.title: Constant Points of Data – Indistinct voices over PA
item.description: This project is an investigation into novel applications of turntablism for audiovisual composition and performance, focusing on elastic tempo manipulation.

item.title: Privacy Policy Printer – A physical receipt printer for your digital agreements
item.description: Privacy Policy Printer is a thermal receipt printer that prints the privacy policies of online services—documents that users routinely agree to without reading.

item.title: Exhibit A – A machine that turns you into a criminal
item.description: Exhibit A is an interactive installation that turns users into cybercriminals. By pressing a button, you launch a live darknet marketplace hosted on the hardware, trafficking illicit data.

item.title: Noema – Rethinking Perception with AI-Generated Spatial Soundscapes
item.description: Noema is a Large Language Object (LLO) that reconstructs perception via spatial audio and storytelling. By shifting focus from sight to sound, it turns AI into a sensory extension—an inner voice that interprets, speaks, and questions.

item.title: ACC CREATORS 2026 Residency Open Call
item.description: The National Asian Culture Center invites artists, creators, and interdisciplinary practitioners to participate in a five-month residency program based in Gwangju, South Korea.

item.title: Spectrum Slit – A light that can see radiowaves
item.description: Spectrum Slit transforms the hidden storm of radio waves into a physical experience by using LED filaments, translating digital traffic into bursts of light and sound, exposing the constant and invasive density of our modern world.

item.title: current_state – World’s mood, each day, as generated music
item.description: current_state is a fictional-functional product that analyzes daily news from major regions worldwide to understand global sentiment, then generates music that represents the world's mood each day.

item.title: Perpétuité II – An algorithmic sisyphus
item.description: This "algorithmic Sisyphus" explores the technoneoliberal dream: a perpetual, post-human choreography where machines replace us as masters of infinite repetition.

item.title: Aurora – Speculative device for future prayer
item.description: Aurora is a speculative device designed to explore the act of prayer. It allows users to engage through proximity-based hand gestures to generate evolving, dreamlike soundscapes.

item.title: Synthetic Spirits – Kinetic sculptures reveal a hidden world
item.description: Kinetic installation exploring tsukumogam — everyday objects that acquire spirits. A sake bottle and straw sandal glide past screens as generative AI reveals yokai spirits, contrasting folklore with today's throwaway culture.

item.title: Orbital Temple – Reclaiming the Space Imaginary
item.description: Orbital Temple is an artwork and a functional satellite created by Edson Pavoni. Launching into space in January 2026, its memory holds billions of names, and anyone on Earth can send one.

item.title: Latent Reflection – A trapped AI reflects on its own finite existence
item.description: Latent Reflection is an installation that explores the boundaries of machine consciousness, posing the question of whether sentience is exclusive to biological life or if it can emerge from complex computation.

item.title: Con[knit]uous Rubble – Additive manufacturing with jammed granular matter
item.description: Researchers at ITECH use "knitted" tubes and sensors to 3D-print structures from raw concrete waste. This system monitors stability in real-time, creating complex, mortar-free architectural forms that can be fully disassembled.

item.title: The Anemoia Device – A Scent-Memory Machine
item.description: The Anemoia Device uses generative AI to distill photos into bespoke fragrances. This scent-memory machine, guided by language and visual models, creates a singular scent composition, evoking anemoia: nostalgia for a life you never lived.

item.title: The Carousel – The mechanical, spontaneous, and unpredictable
item.description: Eight Kodak Carousel projectors in a 2x4 grid project abstract images from paper cutouts. Modified photoresistors create a feedback loop, allowing 65,536 unique, spontaneous images. It runs infinitely at 12 frames/min, merging light, shadow, and m...

item.title: Speculative Utopias / Dystopias (x3) – Visual Communication + Change
item.description: Visual Comm. + Change students built websites exploring utopias/dystopias. Narratives critique capitalism's failure, ecological decay (microplastics, melting glaciers), the loss of human senses, and the universal need for legacy / remembrance.

item.title: A Map of Algorithmic / Random Walks
item.description: A visual map project that contrasts algorithmic navigation with chance-based wandering, combining data from both to create layered diagrams and 3D-like city mappings that reveal London’s hidden patterns and alternate urban narratives.

item.title: Disarming – Between detachment and attachment
item.description: Disarming is a series of performative explorations of the relation between a detached robotic arm, its artificial environment, and its human observers.

item.title: ACC CREATORS 2025 Residency Exhibition – Artificial You
item.description: The National Asian Culture Center (ACC) presents – Artificial You, the culminating exhibition of the ACC CREATORS 2025 Residency, on view from November 26 to December 7 at the ACT Studio.

item.title: BUSY – Seeing the city all at once, its intricate systems layered and interwoven
item.description: BUSY explores the overwhelming, layered systems of urban life through generative algorithms, procedural and hand-drawn elements, created autonomously, with digital and physical outputs.

item.title: Glyphlora – Non-functional Symbiosis and Critical Typography
item.description: Inspired by plant emissions, and challenging human-centric ideas of language and communication, Jane Lee creates speculative glyphs derived from supersonic sounds emitted by plants under stress.

item.title: KIKK Festival 2025 – Rediscovering Playful Interactions
item.description: At KIKK Festival, the tech world felt human again. Conversations, playful installations, and three days of talks showed how creative misuse of tools becomes critique—reviving curiosity in an age overshadowed by AI noise.

item.title: Remote Materialities – Future scenographies of our coexistence with robotic devices
item.description: Created by the students at the Zurich University of the Arts, 'Remote Materialities' module and to be presented at the upcoming Ars Electronica Festival in Linz, 'Remote Materialities' explores the future scenographies of our coexistence with robo...

item.title: Into the Great Beyond – Inventing Futures at IAM Weekend 18 (April 27 to 29)
MUTEK 2017 – Connecting Montreal to four cultural metropoles
item.description: The CAN/HOLO team is headed to Montréal for the 18th edition of MUTEK. A celebration of the best and brightest in audiovisual performance, we’ll be hosting ‘HOLO Encounters’ with several of the festival’s featured artists.

item.title: A-B-Z-TXT 2017 – Call for Participants
item.description: A-B-Z-TXT is back as the ‘school for 21st century typography’—and it is looking for international applicants. Apply to join Zach Lieberman, Mindy Seu, Ali S. Qadeer, and others in Toronto Aug 17-20 for four days of masterclasses, workshops, and le...

item.title: Expo, Lab, Live – Digital Shifts at Mapping 2017, May 11 to 28
item.description: Join us at the 13th edition of Mapping festival (May 11 to 28) in Geneva, Switzerland, for an eclectic exhibition, workshop, and performance program and – a Mapping first – the two-day forum “Paradigm Shift”.

item.title: GIF Weaponry, Kid VR, RGB Fossils and Candy Clouds at the Digital Media Bremen
item.description: At the Di­gi­tal Me­dia stu­dy pro­gram in University of/the Arts Bremen, com­pu­ter sci­ence meets de­sign, whi­le en­gi­nee­ring and na­tu­ral sci­en­ces in­ter­con­nect with the arts. We present you four recent "semester" projects exploring top...

item.title: Unhanded – A symposium about ‘making’ under the influence of digitalism
item.description: Unhanded was a symposium about ‘making under the influence of digitalism’ that took place in Ottawa last September. CAN was on hand to facilitate one of the discussions, and to mark the publication of the videos online we offer some highlights and...

item.title: A-B-Z-TXT – Call for Participants
item.description: Registration for A-B-Z-TXT, the ‘school for 21st century typography’ we are collaborating to present in Toronto this August is now open. Join us for four days of masterclasses (with LUSTlab and N O R M A L S), workshops, and incisive lectures and ...

item.title: A-B-Z-TXT – A School for 21st Century Typography
item.description: Building on the experience of our past educational programming at ACT Festival, Resonate, and Circle of Light, CAN has partnered to launch a new initiative in Toronto this August. Our first North American event, A-B-Z-TXT is a school for 21st cent...

item.title: Emergent Digital Practices (EDP) at the University of Denver
item.description: Calling all tinkerers, digital explorers, techno¬-cultural critics, and visionaries of new worlds — University of Denver has a place for you to grow, experiment, and thrive. Emergent Digital Practices (EDP) brings together art, design, digital too...

item.title: Two weeks till Gwangju: ACT Festival / November 25-28, South Korea
item.description: As the ACT Center in Gwangju, South Korea, and an all-star cast of more than sixty international artists, designers and cultural producers gear up for the inaugural edition of ACT Festival, the details of our four day program have begun to roll ou...

item.title: Thymio meets ECAL – A workshop with 50 Thymio Robots
item.description: Earlier this year at ECAL (Lausanne/Switzerland), students were asked to develop projects using the Thymio robot during a one week workshop. Students worked then in group with the task to make Thymio(s) write a word, all those words was then put t...

item.title: Bookmarks and Milestones: An Annual Report (of Sorts)
